item.title: Former FTX Executive Launches a ‘No Loss’ AI Trading Platform
item.description: At the European arm of failed crypto exchange FTX, Patrick Gruhn watched as traders repeatedly lost money on risky bets. Now, he’s launching a product where the savviest investors can turn a profit — even while putting zero capital at stake.

item.title: Scorching Summer Pushes India’s Power Demand to Record Again
item.description: India’s peak electricity demand climbed to a record for the second consecutive day as blistering heat pushed up use of cooling appliances such as air-conditioners.

item.title: Wall Street Banks Kick Off Loan Sale Tied to Warner Bros. Deal
item.description: Wall Street banks kicked off a loan sale Tuesday for Warner Bros Discovery Inc., with proceeds intended to refinance a temporary credit facility.

item.title: UK On Verge of Trade Deal With Gulf States, Says Head of GCC
item.description: The UK is set to finalize a long-awaited trade deal with the Gulf Cooperation Council on Wednesday, according to Saudi Arabian state media, deepening its ties with the oil-rich nations at a time of rising geopolitical tension and economic pressures.

item.title: Nagel Says ECB May ‘Have to Do Something’ as Iran Shock Persists
item.description: The European Central Bank may have to respond to the economic challenges arising from the conflict in the Middle East, according to Governing Council member Joachim Nagel.

item.title: Sherritt Halts Plan to Unwind Cuba Ventures Amid Sanctions Talks
item.description: Sherritt International Corp. reversed course on plans to unwind its Cuban business after further consultations with advisers and government officials, as the nickel producer scrambles to respond to tougher US sanctions on the island.

item.title: Canada Inflation Rises to 2.8% While Core Measures Ease
item.description: Higher energy prices drove up Canada’s inflation rate to 2.8% in April, reaching its highest level in nearly two years but coming in lower than economists had expected as core measures cooled.

item.title: G-7 Urges Fiscal Restraint in Face of War Shock to Economies
item.description: Group of Seven finance chiefs pledged not to overdo any fiscal aid as the Iran war raises growth and inflation risks to the global economy.

item.title: Why the Iran War Is a Boost for Stalled Russia-China Gas Pipeline
item.description: The Iran war and resulting energy crisis could inject new urgency into plans for a major natural gas pipeline between Russia and China.

item.title: Senegal Arrears to Arab Development Bank Highlight Debt Woes
item.description: The Arab Bank for Economic Development in Africa told investors last week that Senegal is in arrears with the lender, people familiar with the matter said, adding to signs of mounting financial strain on the government. The nation’s dollar bonds fell.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 19, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 0.4% as of 7:42 a.m. in New York as investors weigh the balances of market positioning, profit taking, rotation and dip buying in the absence of significant macro events since the close on Monday.

item.title: Russian Oil Flows Rebound as Key Black Sea Port Fully Restarted
item.description: Loading resumes through all berths at Novorossiysk crude oil terminal

item.title: NextEra’s Mega-Deal Heralds the Age of the Battery
item.description: The US utility is homing in on energy storage as ballooning data-center growth drives demand.

item.title: New York and US Northeast Brace for Extreme Heat, Power Alert
item.description: New Yorkers face a sweltering Tuesday commute as temperatures soar, threatening to topple regional records and strain energy supplies.

item.title: US Stock Futures Drop as Semiconductor Stocks Slip, Yields Climb
item.description: US futures ticked lower with 10-year Treasury yields climbing above 4.6%. Major semiconductor stocks like Micron Technology Inc. and Nvidia Corp. extended declines.

item.title: Orient Express Rail Operator Seeks €300 Million From Debut Bond
item.description: The company behind the Orient Express in Italy is planning a €300 million ($349 million) bond sale to refinance debt as it seeks to refurbish some of its assets.

item.title: AI Rollout to Boost Metals-Rich EM Currencies, Barclays Says
item.description: Currencies of emerging-market nations that produce the metals and minerals key to artificial intelligence are set to get a boost in the coming years, according to Barclays Plc.

item.title: AI Moving From ‘Hype’ to Real Execution, JPMorgan’s Brunner Says
item.description: Enthusiasm for artificial intelligence is no longer just rosy predictions about the future, with the technology now making significant real-world impacts, according to Kevin Brunner, JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s global chair of investment banking and mergers and acquisitions.

item.title: Indonesia Plans to Tighten State Control Over Commodity Exports
item.description: Indonesia plans to tighten control over commodity exports including coal and palm oil, according to people familiar with the matter, as the government seeks to clamp down on tax evasion and bolster a plunging rupiah.

item.title: French Socialists to Oppose Moulin as Bank of France Head
item.description: Socialist lawmakers will oppose Emmanuel Macron’s nominee for Bank of France Governor, raising the risks of a political blow for the French president and uncertainty over the composition of the European Central Bank’s Governing Council.

item.title: Target’s Stock Rally Raises Earnings Bar as Comeback Gains Steam
item.description: Target Corp. has quietly emerged as one of this year’s hottest retail stocks, outperforming many staples and big-box peers. The rally has also raised the bar heading into quarterly results.

item.title: Meta Bets $200 Billion It Can Show Massive AI Data Center Will Pay Off
item.description: Investors want to see whether the company’s huge facility in rural Louisiana is worth the cost

item.title: Nutrition Care Startup Raises $100 Million to Help GLP-1 Treatment
item.description: Nourish, a nutrition counseling startup, has raised $100 million in a new round of funding to help people make dietary changes while taking GLP-1s, with the goal of boosting and preserving improvements from the weight-loss medications.

item.title: The EU Is Seeking to Ease Trade Strains With US and China
item.description: The world’s three major economic engines are jockeying to reset their trading relationships

item.title: Fashion Giant Loved by Hollywood Stars Crashes in Brazil Markets
item.description: To the outside world, Brazilian fashion giant Azzas 2154 SA appears to be having a breakout moment. Its latest winter campaign featured Sarah Jessica Parker; Meryl Streep turned up to a premiere of The Devil Wears Prada 2 in its black Scarpin heels; and Justin Bieber has been seen sporting its kaleidoscopic puffer jacket.

item.title: Bond Markets Give Yippy Vibes Just as Kevin Warsh Is Set to Become Fed Chair
item.description: A selloff in US Treasuries has taken some yields to their highest in years.

item.title: Ecarx, Backed by Geely’s Li, to Sell Robotaxis to US Startup
item.description: Ecarx Holdings Inc., a company backed by Chinese billionaire Li Shufu, said it reached an agreement to deliver robotaxis to US startup May Mobility Inc., in a deal it valued at about $750 million.

item.title: US Reaches Deep Into Asia for Supply-Chain Sovereignty
item.description: The Beijing summit hasn’t stopped the race for AI dominance

item.title: Bond Market’s Woes Go Far Beyond the Price of Oil
item.description: Demographics, debt, AI also weigh on sovereign bonds

item.title: China’s YMTC Kicks Off IPO Process to Tap Booming Memory Demand
item.description: Yangtze Memory Technologies Co. has begun the process for a long-awaited initial public offering, tapping red-hot demand for the chipmakers at the heart of a global AI development boom.
